response.justification: Strong software craftsman with 20 years of experience and excellent distributed systems background, but the resume shows fundamental misalignment with this industrial C++ role. C++ expertise is severely dated (last work December 2014–June 2015, 11 years ago), and career trajectory clearly moved away from embedded systems toward web and full-stack development. Critical gaps include no industrial automation domain experience, no IEC 62443 or secure development lifecycle expertise, no MQTT or industrial protocol knowledge, and no message broker architecture design for industrial contexts. While the candidate demonstrates strong fundamentals, onboarding would require extensive C++ ramp-up and domain knowledge acquisition—a significant risk for a mid-senior role.
strengths: Exceptional software craftsmanship: TDD, design patterns, code quality, and emphasis on testability align with secure development and quality mindset
Proven ability with distributed systems and high-performance concurrent architectures (micro-services handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby)
Strong technical communication and mentoring demonstrated through workshops, conference talks, and open-source framework contributions
concerns: C++ expertise is severely outdated (last work 11 years ago for 6 months) and career trajectory moved away from systems programming toward web and full-stack development
Zero experience in industrial automation, manufacturing domain, or industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443, SDL)—critical for this role
No demonstrated expertise with message brokers, MQTT protocol, or industrial communication protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA, DNP 3.0)

key_factors.factor: Industrial automation and manufacturing domain knowledge
Software craftsmanship and code quality discipline
